items.title: Designing classifier head width
items.description: Image and text classifiers set the output layer width to the number of classes (10 for MNIST, 1000 for ImageNet). Each output neuron corresponds to one logit or probability mass for that class.

items.title: Capacity tuning via hidden width
items.description: Practitioners sweep hidden layer widths (32, 64, 128, 256) on validation data to balance underfitting and overfitting. Wider hidden layers increase parameters and representational capacity at each stage.

items.title: Depth for hierarchical features
items.description: Vision and speech pipelines stack multiple hidden layers so early tiers capture simple patterns and later tiers compose them. Depth is the primary architectural knob for hierarchical representation learning in dense nets.

items.title: Reading model summaries and FLOP estimates
items.description: Tools like torchinfo, TensorBoard, and ONNX analyzers report layer names, input/output shapes, and parameter counts derived from width and connectivity. Understanding layers and neurons lets you interpret those summaries before training.
